Construction status: Land clearing begins next to the existing building.

Battery facility connected to the building. 121 air-cooled chillers installed, which could support 200 MW. In the Duke faciltiy in nearby Mississippi, several natural gas turbines are in place but not fully connected up.

Battery facility looks complete (though more might be added). Turbines look connected up, minimal construction around them. We believe this state corresponds to the first compute cluster brought online at this site of "approximately 110,000 GB200 processors, approximately 210 megawatts of compute power", as detailed in the [company S-1 filing, page 76](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm). Based on those numbers, we believe "compute power" corresponds to GPU server power. [Earlier](https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1947715674429919279) [tweets](https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1950251710141567110) from Elon Musk also support this timing, and it's consistent with the estimated cooling capacity based on the satellite image.

Since October, an array of dry cooling air-cooled condensers has been added (the array of large fans to the left of the building), and appears almost complete. Two more similar arrays appear to be under construction. More air-cooled chillers, batteries, and gas turbines have also been installed.

The first array of dry cooling air-cooled condensers for Building 1 has been completed and is operational. A second array, with larger fans, is about halfway complete (located to the south of the first array). More chillers have also been added. For Building 2, an array of dry cooling air-cooled condensers of the same type as Building 1's first array is beginning construction. We believe this corresponds to the "second cluster of 110,000 GB300 processors and 220 megawatts of compute power" from the [company S-1 filing, page 76](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm), with compute power corresponding to GPU server power. This is also consistent with our estimates based on cooling equipment.

The site is estimated to have at least ~830 MW of GPU server power, with at least "at least 220,000 additional GB300 processors and over 400 additional megawatts of compute power", corresponding to the "next phase of expansion" found in the following [company S-1 filing, page 76](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm). The timing is based on the air-cooled condensers needing to be complete to handle this power capacity. The second array at MACROHARD and the first array at MACROHARDRR, although both projected to be completed prior, are not enough and more cooling capacity is needed. We estimate this expansion to be completed 90 days after April 6th. On one hand, SpaceXAI has progressively added more capacity in less time with their Colossus projects. On the other hand, 400 MW is more capacity than the previous expansions of 210 MW and 220 MW. We also believe that MACROHARDRR will be partly operational with this expansion, because its air-cooled condensor array is expected to be completed by then and fitting the entire expansion into only MACROHARD would result in extremely high levels of power density.

Buildings 19-32 are operational. This is an estimate based on how long Buildings 1-16 took to become operational. In May 2026, the foundation of several buildings and a substation was visible at the site of Buildings 19-32. Buildings 1-16 took 1 year and 10 months to go from a similar state to operational. Land clearing begins for northern two buildings (Building 1-2)

Building 1 looks close to finishing construction, with chillers in place. OpenAI [stated](https://openai.com/index/five-new-stargate-sites/) that "Oracle began delivering the first NVIDIA GB200 racks in June." The "tenant improvement" work [is not due](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025000156) until September, however.

Building 1 is fully operational around this date. The first phase was "live" on September 30 according a Crusoe [announcement](https://www.crusoe.ai/resources/newsroom/crusoe-announces-flagship-abilene-data-center-is-live). While the first phase consists of Building 1 and 2, the language doesn't outright confirm that both buildings are fully operational. The announcement also stated that "Oracle began delivering the first NVIDIA GB200 racks in June", and we think 3 months is enough time to ram to 50,000 GB200s in Building 1. The satellite image also suggests that Building 1 is complete (shell and equipment done, many cars neatly parked) while Building 2 is ongoing (trucks and construction equipment still present). The 200 MW [substation](https://www.mortenson.com/projects/abilene-data-center-development) built in 2022 and expanded in 2024 is enough to power both buildings. To the right of Building 2, five gas turbines are in place with five more turbines still under construction. Chillers are partly installed for Building 3. Roof is complete for Building 4. The larger 1 GW substation is not complete yet.

Building 2 is fully operational. This exact date corresponds to the expected completion date of "Four (4) data hall spaces and an administrative office build-out" according to a [TDLR filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025007742), but this date is also consistent with satellite imagery. By January 15, Building 2 looked as finished as Building 1, whereas on September 26 it did not look finished.

The 1 GW substation looks close to completion. Building 3 appears to have all power and cooling equipment installed, but there are still signs of construction work. This seems on track for operation in 3 months. Building 4 has about 80% of chillers installed. Other buildings have few or no chillers installed.

Building 3 and 4 look complete, but are not fully operational. Not as many cars are parked next to it as Building 1-2 which suggests they may still be ramping up. Building 4 was due by now according to a [regulatory filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025014756). Building 5 still has light construction activity, while Building 6 still lacks chillers. The on-site power plant to the right of Building 2 looks complete.

Buildings 3 and 4 are estimated to be operational. On April 22nd, Oracle [posted](https://x.com/i/status/2047012589302153558) that "In Abilene, 200MW is already operational". This suggests that there was still only Building 1 and 2 operational. Since Buildings 3 and 4 looked complete from the outside on March 24th, we give it another month after Oracle's post for them to be operational. This would also give the buildings a similar 1 year 4 months timeline that Buildings 1 took from land clearing to being operational.

Buildings 5-8 are estimated to be operational. On March 24, Building 8 roughly looked like where Building 3 was in September. Building 3 is estimated to be operational in April, 7 months later. This naively suggests Building 8 will be operational by early November, 7 months after March 24.

The substation is still under construction, based on a [Google Earth image](https://earth.google.com/web/@37.48033002,-77.25002949,65.53442036a,2152.9927926d,30.00094375y,-0.00000001h,0.59169466t,0r/data=CgwqBggBEgAYAUICCAEyKQonCiUKITFZU1hJZlBmRzJ1dUxibWdQOTJzY05sTVB6cXVUZFVXZyABOgMKATBCAggASggIw4mB_QIQAQ), so we don't think any buildings are operational yet.
The substation looks complete, but we expect Building 1 still needs commissioning before it is fully operational.

Building 1 is operational, based on the substation being completed earlier and cars in the carpark. Building 2 and 3 are not operational, based on minor construction work at the perimeter and no cars in the carpark, but we expect they will be operational within 3 months. Building 4 has complete chillers and backup generators, while Building 5 has chillers but only a few backup generators - roughly the state of Building 1 on 2025-02-04.
Building 2 and 3 are operational, based on the previous entry.
Building 4 and 5 are operational, based on Building 5 being roughly 1 year behind Building 1 in satellite imagery.
Land clearing begins.
Building 1 and 2 under construction, with all chillers visible.

Buildings 1 and 2 are nearly complete, but there's still some equipment strewn around the site and few cars in the front carpark, so we expect 3 more months needed for commissioning and server installation. Building 3 has a half-finished roof and Building 4 shell is beginning construction.
Building 1 and 2 are operational (estimate).
Buildings 5 and 6 are operational (estimate). Based on the tent buildings at Meta Prometheus taking about 11 months from starting the tent structure to estimated operation.

Buildings 3 and 4 are operational (estimate). In September 2025, Building 1 and 2 appeared to be in a similar state to Building 3 and 4 in May 2026. Based on that, we apply an 8-month delay to the Building 1 and 2 operational date to get the Building 3 and 4 operational date.
Land clearing begins.

Building 1 and 2 look complete from outside, with all electrical and cooling equipment installed. Construction has started on 2 smaller buldings to the north. Meta announced the arrival of the first server rack the following week.

Building 1 and 2 are operational. The 2nd phase of an offsetting solar array goes online on May 31, 2026.  Idaho Public Utilities Comission's approval for this start date notes that "[Meta]’s load is expected to increase so much that it will suffer a capacity deficit in June 2026 if served only by Pleasant Valley Solar 1.1,” A redacted table in the staff comments document suggests the data center's load will continue to increase through August, when it presumably reaches the maximum capacity of both buildings.
Land clearing begins.
A traditional H-shaped Meta data center is partially built.
The traditional Meta data center has been demolished mid-construction, in favor of Meta's new AI-optimized design.
Building 1 and 2 look complete from outside, with all electrical and cooling equipment installed. Space is cleared for Building 3.

Building 1 and 2 are operational. Activity related to the air quality permit (namely, emergency diesel generators) was reported to have started as of 2025-12-09. This is our best estimate, but note that the planned completion date in this [regulatory filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2022017728) is 2026-03-01.
Building 3 is operational. Based on the stated completion date in this [regulatory filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2026008019)
Land clearing begins
Roof complete on Buildings 3
Buildings 1-4 are operational, based on the buildings shells and backup generators and most of the landscaping looking complete. Building 5 roof is complete but still lacks cooling equipment.
Building 5 is operational (estimate).

Buildings 6-14 are operational (estimate). Buildings 1-4 took an estimated 2.25 years on this site. We assume Buildings 6-14 get underway by July 2026, and take the same time as Buildings 1-4. This is later than the [expected completion by 2027](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/aws-confirmed-as-company-behind-10bn-mississippi-data-center-development/).
Land clearing begins
Buildings 1 and 2 are nearly complete, but there's still some equipment strewn around the site. We expect 3 more months needed for commissioning and server installation.
Building 1 and 2 are operational (estimate).

The former [crypto mining](https://investor.galaxy.com/news/news-details/2025/Galaxy-Announces-Commitment-with-CoreWeave-to-Host-Additional-Artificial-Intelligence-and-High-Performance-Computing-Infrastructure-at-Helios-Data-Center-Campus/default.aspx) Helios site begins expanding, with land cleared for additional buildings.
Backup generator pads and steel framing for chillers are visible, similar to the [3D renderings](https://www.galaxy.com/data-centers).

Building 1 appears substantially complete. The main white-roofed data hall is visible with exterior mechanical/electrical equipment rows installed on both sides, and adjacent generator/equipment pads plus laydown yards are active. A large graded pad north of Building 1 suggests preparation for the next phase/building. No clear satellite evidence that the AI/HPC facility is operational yet.

Building 1 operational at full capacity. This corresponds to "Phase I", which is [expected](https://investor.galaxy.com/news/news-details/2025/Galaxy-Announces-Third-Quarter-2025-Financial-Results/default.aspx) to be operational by the end of the first half of 2026.

Building 2 and 3 are operational at full capacity. This corresponds to "Phase II", which is [expected](https://investor.galaxy.com/news/news-details/2025/Galaxy-Announces-Third-Quarter-2025-Financial-Results/default.aspx) to be delivered "throughout 2027". We assume it takes until the end of 2027 to reach full capacity.

Building 4 operational at full capacity. This corresponds to "Phase III", which is [expected](https://investor.galaxy.com/news/news-details/2025/Galaxy-Announces-Third-Quarter-2025-Financial-Results/default.aspx) to be delivered "starting in 2028". We assume it takes until the end of 2028 to reach full capacity.
Building 1 foundation started
Building 1 roof complete, cooling towers in progress.
Building 1 appears almost complete, with minimal construction activity.
Building 1 is operational, based off of colored motion artifacts from cooling towers.
Land clearing begins on the north part of the site
Building 1 and 1st building on the site are operational. Foundation work begins on Building 2
Building 2 is operational. Estimated based on Microsoft H100 count 2023
Building 3-4 operational, no visible construction.
Site already complete.
No notable changes since October.

Building 1, which was first operational in 2021, has increased power capacity by about 75%. This is based on the expansion of the dry cooling array from 4 to 7 columns (seen on [Google Earth](https://earth.google.com/web/@39.4948785,-119.43174359,1568.8659293a,562.69639351d,35y,-0h,0t,0r/)). Its backup generators also grew from 18 to 33, an 83% increase. Around this time (2023 Q3) the quarterly PUE of the campus is 1.24, down from the initial 1.29 in 2022 Q3. Since no other buildings have been added, these changes indicate an increase in power density, which may entail a new generation of hardware getting installed.
Building 2 now has generators in place, but there is still a crane and construction work present.

Building 2 is operational. Based on a [Google Earth image](https://earth.google.com/web/@39.49733738,-119.43113543,1552.05877612a,1467.7067385d,35y,0h,0t,0r/) showing complete building shell, mechanical equipment and generators, plus cars parked at the front.
Building 3 foundation is underway. Building 2 exterior looks totally clean/landscaped.
Building 3 has a partially complete shell, electrical and mechanical equipment. Building 4 foundation is underway.

Building 3 is estimated to be operational. We estimate the operational date by analogy to the time that Building 1 took to go from a foundation to being operational. The power capacity is based on the number of generators reported in an air quality permit [application](https://ndep-onbase.nv.gov/api/Document/AeFo%C3%89pjclbkomJ7CWpPryLOjA6FQT9HFrkSuMWbMXTpKCzSR%C3%81W1f8zzFhGhik1iZCcNSpuTRRqNXD1inrrpBxtA%3D/) (p.92-93). The dry cooler array is more than double the size of the Building 1 array, which suggests an even larger power capacity than we estimate, but we believe the generator-based estimate is more reliable here.
Land clearing begins.

We believe Buildings 1 and 2 are still undergoing testing due to construction equipment and unfinished land surrounding the data center sites, as well as color motion artifacts on a small number of the cooling fans.
Buildings 1 and 2 both estimated to be operational 90 days after testing was underway in September.
Satellite image shows strong evidence that Buildings 1 and 2 are operational: colored motion artifacts on many cooling fans, plus cars parked at the building entrances.
Land clearing begins for Building 1, also known as CB-1, the first AI/HPC building on the site.

Building 1 is operational. Based on Terawulf [saying](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/126/terawulf-reports-third-quarter-2025-results) "As of September 30, 2025, the site has energized 245 MW of Bitcoin-mining capacity and 22.5 MW of HPC capacity." The small "Wulf Den" building was [said](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/99/terawulf-to-deliver-over-70-mw-of-data-center) to be 2 MW of IT power while CB-1 would be 16 MW. This is roughly consistent with 22.5 MW of total facility power. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/99/terawulf-to-deliver-over-70-mw-of-data-center) to Core42, a subsidiary of UAE-based G42. It likely uses NVIDIA DGX B200 servers.

Buildings 1-3 all appear to have a complete building and cooling equipment. Building 4 shell is still under construction, but 17 cooling units are already loaded on the roof. The outline of Building 5 is visible at the foundation.

Building 2 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB2A is operational, presumably making up half the final capacity. CB2B was expected in March 2026. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/99/terawulf-to-deliver-over-70-mw-of-data-center) to Core42, a subsidiary of UAE-based G42. It likely uses NVIDIA DGX B200 servers.

Building 3 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB3 was expected to be energized by mid-May 2026. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/112/terawulf-signs-200-mw-10-year-ai-hosting-agreements-with) to Fluidstack, likely contains [Google TPUs](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-offers-its-tpus-to-ai-cloud-providers-report/), and is likely used by [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-invests-50-billion-in-american-ai-infrastructure).

Building 4 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB4 was expected to be energized by Q3 2026. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/112/terawulf-signs-200-mw-10-year-ai-hosting-agreements-with) to Fluidstack, likely contains [Google TPUs](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-offers-its-tpus-to-ai-cloud-providers-report/), and is likely used by [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-invests-50-billion-in-american-ai-infrastructure).

Building 5 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB5 was expected to be energized by Q4 2026. However, based on the pace of Building 4, and comparisons to other projects like Stargate Abilene, we think this is optimistic. We expect a 3-month delay past the end of Q4 2026 to get to full operational capacity. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/112/terawulf-signs-200-mw-10-year-ai-hosting-agreements-with) to Fluidstack, likely contains [Google TPUs](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-offers-its-tpus-to-ai-cloud-providers-report/), and is likely used by [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-invests-50-billion-in-american-ai-infrastructure).

Building 1 operational. This is estimated based on construction progress in [October 2023](https://earth.google.com/web/@39.72490515,-82.68845499,280.4138206a,1128.806013d,35y,-0h,0t,0r/data=CnUaXRJXCiUweDg4NDc4OWI3MDRhZGE1MWQ6MHg0Y2Q1MjE2ZGM0YTQ0YTc5GSZeVEH73ENAISDkBo2qq1TAKhwxMDQgV2hpbGV5IFJkLCBMYW5jYXN0ZXIsIE9IGAIgASoQCAESCjIwMjMtMDUtMTAYAUICCAFCAggASg0I____________ARAA). Building 2 is close to completion. Structurally it is already complete, but there is still some equipment strewn around the building that suggests commissioning is ongoing.
Building 2 operational. This is supported by the state of Building 2 a month prior, which is entirely complete outside of some equipment that was removed later on.
At this point, Building 1 and 2 have since already been operational. Additionally, there is visible steam showing clear evidence of operational for both before this date.
Land clearing begins for the entire site.

Building 1 is operational. This is based on the state of construction on 2025-03-02 as seen on [Google Earth](https://earth.google.com/web/@33.35450568,-111.67805903,407.02398359a,2119.1496037d,35y,-0h,0t,0r/data=ChYqEAgBEgoyMDI1LTAzLTAyGAFCAggBQgIIAEoNCP___________wEQAA), where building shell and external components seems to have been completed, but there is still a lot of construction equipment present. That equipment appears to have been cleared up by this date, 4 months later, with the 2025-09-07 satellite imagery providing an upper bound. Construction has begun on the Building 2 roof, with work beginning on the areas for cooling and generators as well.
Building 2 roof is mostly completed. Building 2's backup generators are almost complete, and cooling is a little more than halfway complete. The Building 2 substation is also under construction.

Building 2 is close to being operational. There is only construction left on the backup generators, which are almost complete. Some equipment is also strewn around the building, especially near the air-cooled chillers. Preparation seems to have begun for Building 3 at the site.
Building 2 is operational. This is estimated based on the close-to-complete construction status from May 2026.
Land clearing begins (Sentinel-2 imagery).

Building complete and operational; full buildout reached. Sentinel-2 imagery shows the building finished with components installed and cars in the car park. Confirmed by Google's official blog post announcing the Waltham Cross data centre opening (https://blog.google/company-news/inside-google/around-the-globe/google-europe/united-kingdom/waltham-cross-data-centre/).
Full buildout reconfirmed via high-resolution Pléiades-Neo satellite image; no capacity change since September 2025.

Land clearing begins simultaneously for all three zones (west, north, and south) after an [official ceremony](https://web.archive.org/web/20260624112951/http://nmg.news.cn/20231008/57bb95be6791497db95bc7d2f229a68e/c.html). Two of the zones later have the cooling capacity indicative of AI workloads.
Building 1 is structurally complete, and building 4 is structurally complete apart from some remaining cooling towers. Buildings 2 and 5 have begun construction.
4 structure complete via satellite imagery
Building 1 is operational, assuming a 3 month fit out post structural completion, as can be seen via satellite imagery.
Building 4 is operational, assuming a 3 month fit out post structural completion, as can be seen via satellite imagery.
Building 2 is operational, assuming a 3 month fit out post structural completion, as can be seen via satellite imagery.

Three of the six (phase 1) buildings planned across the three zones are operational. One other is near completion, apart from backup generators. The third zone does not have cooling sufficient for AI.
Satellite imagery shows buildings 4 and 5 are all structurally finished, with landscaping complete.
Satellite imagery shows buildings 1, 2, 3, and 4 are all finished, with landscaping complete.
Building 5 is operational, assuming a 3 month fit out post structural completion, as can be seen via satellite imagery.

Land clearing begins late March, as can be seen in satellite images (eastern part: buildings 1 & 2). VNET officially [announces](https://web.archive.org/web/20250620154431/https://www.vnet.com/portal/article/index/cid/14/id/876.html) its signing ceremony with the Ulanqab municipal government and Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings.
Satellite imagery shows five buildings (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) under construction. Chillers are in place, but no roof is complete for any building.
Building 1 is operational, based on a 3-month fit-out period after structural completion in April, as can be seen from satellite imagery.

Satellite imagery shows that in total nine buildings have started construction, with building 1 being the only one operational. Buildings 2 and 5 are structurally complete, and the final roofs for buildings 3 and 4 have begun.

Buildings 2 and 5 are operational, based on a 3-month fit-out period after structural completion in August, as can be seen from satellite imagery. Land clearing for the northern part of campus (buildings 10 and 11) has started.
Building 4 is operational, similarly based on a 3-month fit-out period after structural completion in September, as seen from satellite imagery.

Satellite imagery shows four operational buildings (1, 2, 4, 5), and the western part of campus (6, 7, 8, 9) has made significant construction progress. The foundation for the northern part of campus (10, 11) has started.

Satellite imagery shows building 9 has completed all its chillers, with only its roof remaining. Previous building completion timelines would imply this building will be operational by the end of 2026.

Estimated: the nine-building section of the campus is projected to be fully operational by late 2027 or early 2028. VNET's eastern cluster (buildings 1-5) took roughly a year each from clearing to operational based on satellite imagery. The western cluster (buildings 6-9) was cleared in late 2024 but as of mid-2026 is not yet structurally complete, putting each on track for ~2 years from clearing to operational. Buildings 10 and 11 are projected to be completed sometime later. Comparable data centers in Ulanqab are expected to take at least 6 months per phase ([China Unicom](https://archive.ph/bJDHl)).
First signs of development/land clearing

Building 1 is operational, based on a Google Earth [image](https://earth.google.com/web/@32.53855187,-96.7936939,182.71625265a,4648.56607908d,30.00094375y,3.66969189h,0t,0r/) showing a completed building shell and equipment, and the large construction car park being emptied. Building 2 shell looks mostly completed.

Building 1 appears active - the surrounding land is finished including the site entrance and the parking lot. The dry coolers for Building 2 are nearly complete although the building is still clearly a construction zone. The Building 2 substation also appears complete.
Building 2 is estimated to be operational.
Land clearing starts for Building 1.
Building 1 shell and cooling looks complete, but construction equipment still seen around cooling and building. We estimate another 4 months to operational.
Building 1 is operational (estimate).

Building 1 looks operational.

Start of construction for the first building, according to [Applied Digital](https://ir.applieddigital.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/75/applied-digital-announces-groundbreaking-of). CoreWeave was not leased yet at this site, but this is the construction start date we're using for relevance.

CoreWeave signed the first lease package for 250 MW. No buildings online yet. This is according to an [SEC 8-k filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1144879/000164117225013199/form8-k.htm) from this date.

[A new 150 MW lease](https://ir.applieddigital.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/128/applied-digital-finalizes-additional-150mw-lease-with) brings CoreWeave to 400 MW total contracted. The project is now clearly scheduled to be a three-building CoreWeave campus.

The first 50 MW slice of Building 1 has reached Ready for Service status according to [this report](https://ir.applieddigital.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/133/applied-digital-achieves-ready-for-service-for-phase-1-at). This is the first point where we would count any CoreWeave capacity as operational.

[This Applied Digital report](https://ir.applieddigital.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/137/applied-digital-completes-phase-ii-ready-for-service-at) says the rest of Building 1 has reached Ready for Service, bringing the first building to its expected 100 MW. We can treat this as the first completed data center building.

According to satellite imagery, Building 1 looks complete and in service, with the surrounding power/cooling equipment also operational. (Note: this image doesn't provide a view of buildings 2 or 3, though it's very safe to assume they're under construction at this stage, considering they're still under construction in the satellite image 3 months from now.)

According to satellite imagery, Building 2 is fully roofed and shell complete, with rooftop chillers mostly installed. Building 3 construction is well underway, with about 1/4 of the roofing done and the foundation laid out.

According to the [schedule](https://ir.applieddigital.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/128/applied-digital-finalizes-additional-150mw-lease-with), Building 2 should be online sometime in the second half of 2026. We can estimate the end of 2026 for 250 MW operational.

According to [schedule](https://ir.applieddigital.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/128/applied-digital-finalizes-additional-150mw-lease-with), Building 3 should come online in the first half of 2027. That would bring the full PF1 campus to 400 MW, according to the contract.

Conversion starts at CTP-01 (Building 1). The data center was formerly owned by Capital One, now leased to CoreWeave. Source: https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/chirisa-secures-200-million-loan-to-fit-out-virginia-data-center-for-coreweave/
Building 1's second data hall scheduled to come online this month.
Building 1 is scheduled to be fully online by this point, so I count the full 27 MW here.
Satellite imagery shows Building 1 complete, with Building 2's yard work and early construction visible. Building 3 hasn't started yet, since the old building is still there.
Based on satellite imagery, Building 3 has cleared its land and is beginning construction. Building 2 is operational judging from the colored motion artifacts on the cooling tower fans.
Buildings 3 is estimated complete by the end of Q1 2027, based on the current PowerHouse schedule. Added another three months to account for commissioning and server installation.
Land clearing begins.

Building 1 and 2 are operational. Based on Oracle [announcing](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/releasenotes/oci/new-region-batam.htm) the Indonesia North (Batam) region is available, and both buildings looking complete soon after, in June.
Building 3 shell is partly built. Building 1 and 2 look complete.
Building 3 has cooling towers beginning to be installed.
Building 3 looks complete including building shell, chillers and backup generators.

Building 3 is operational. Based on it looking complete as of March, and DayOne [posting](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/dayonedc-datacenter-batam-ugcPost-7450899048925986816-Wxqu/) a video of the facility on this date. In one shot of the video, fan movement is visible for all buildings, suggesting they are all operational.
The three buildings look unchanged since March.

Core Scientific announced that CoreWeave would receive about 70 MW of HPC infrastructure from 100 MW of owned infrastructure. Site modifications were expected to begin in H2 2024.
Marble had achieved full site energization. Two of three data halls had been delivered at 36 billable MW, supporting about 15000 Grace Blackwell GPUs. The third hall was still in commissioning.
Satellite imagery shows the main data hall and southern utility yard substantially complete. No new public MW disclosure, so this keeps the March 2026 public 36 MW state.
Core Scientific said Marble was fully handed over to CoreWeave with about 65 MW of billable customer power online.

Land clearing begins for the site, specifically digging up a parking lot and making outlines for the buildings. Evident on [Google Earth](https://earth.google.com/web/@29.4149522,-98.80304495,292.48816289a,1532.49619452d,35y,0h,0t,0r/).
Building 1 operational. Estimated 3 months out from February, when there was still construction equipment.
Building 2 operational. No more construction on building, and surrounding roads are also complete.
Site has already been completed, with no more visible construction on any building.

Land clearing begins by Peterson Companies for Stack Infrastructure, which is noted in the [news](https://www.bisnow.com/washington-dc/news/data-center/peterson-cos-partnering-to-build-4m-sf-data-center-campus-in-manassas-102646) and visible on satellite imagery.

Building 1 is operational, which is assumed to be three months after the last backup diesel generators are finished. The generators were first seen to be finished July 1 from satellite imagery [(a clear image of this can be seen on July 29 via Google Earth).](https://earth.google.com/web/search/38%c2%b044%2745%2e12%22N+77%c2%b032%2716%2e47%22W/@38.7458667,-77.5379083,83.89135729a,642.01325639d,35y,0h,0t,0r/data=CjQiJgokCTo0Rh9P7TVAETk0Rh9P7TXAGX6C6a6li0VAIQ_0AAa-ik7AKgYIARIAGAFCAggBQgIIAEoNCP___________wEQAA)
All buildings have begun construction (with groundwork starting for AI building 6), as depicted by satellite imagery.

Estimate: All 6 AI data center buildings will be finished in 2028, assuming a groundbreak-to-operational timeline of 18-24 months. One is complete, three are clearly under construction, and the last two data centers are still breaking ground and laying foundations.
Construction beginning, as can be seen on satellite. Major renovations on western side of the non AI building (PX1), including adding generators and cooling towers.
Major construction has finished on AI building 1, and AI building 6 has been expedited, as depicted by satellite imagery.

Estimated: AI building 1 should be operational. Major construction work finished by January 31, 2026, and the shell looked complete by that date. We estimate after three months the building will be operational.

Although official [promotional sources](https://www.streamdatacenters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/SDC-Goodyear-CaseStudy-251218.pdf) estimate the campus to be fully operational in 10-12 years, it is likely to be finished sooner than this given the empirical durations for other data center campuses as well as because AI building 6 has been expedited. The first AI building took approximately 24 months to complete. Assuming construction for a new building starts midway for every building, then the campus will be complete in 4 years for the 5 remaining buildings, since AI buildings 2 and 6 are both underway.
Land clearing for the site is almost complete. Informed using imagery on [Google Earth](https://earth.google.com/web/@29.476115,-98.68118,277.21295794a,724.52967601d,35y,0h,0t,0r/)

Building 1 operational. The first 2 data halls of Building 1 (with chillers already installed) are likely to be already operational. Although construction was completed prior, the on-site substation was previously incomplete, so we believe it was only operational at this point.
Building 2 chiller installation is a little more than halfway complete, and chiller installation for the last data hall of Building 1 is also around halfway complete.

Building 2 fully operational, Building 1 expanded. Construction is already visibly complete. According to a [few](https://investors.corescientific.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/74/core-scientific-to-provide-approximately-200-mw-of-infrastructure-to-host-coreweaves-high-performance-computing-services-capturing-significant-ai-compute-opportunity) [sources](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1839341/000162828025008302/core-20241231.htm), Core Scientific announced that they're rebuilding their existing Dalton 1 (and we're assuming 2) datacenter to run AI using CoreWeave's GPUs, of which contract was first signed on this date. According to the press release, "The site modifications are expected to commence early in the second half of 2024 and achieve operational status in the first half of 2025."

Core Scientific investor presentation clearly shows Dalton 1 with a cleared pad on this month for construction. Source: https://investors.corescientific.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001140361-25-039864/0001140361-25-039864.pdf
Based on Satellite imagery, Dalton 1 is still under construction. The building appears mostly roofed except for the entrance area, and there are no cooling units or backup generators installed yet.

Building 1 is operational, given it was announced completed 4 months ago and looks completed in satellite imagery. All cooling/mechanical rows are installed, with no obvious active building construction visible.
New chiller configuration starts getting installed on Building 1-3.

Large substation is visibly connected to transmission lines. New chillers look fully installed on Building 1-3. Since power and cooling is apparently in place, we expect Building 1-3 are operational. Chillers are almost installed on Building 4-6, with construction equipment still around the building.

Chillers look fully installed on Building 4-6. Some chillers on Building 4 and 5 appear to have snow in the fan holes, suggesting they are not operating and other chillers are operating (since warm air would melt the snow). There is still a bit of construction equipment around Building 6 so it may not be quite operational.
Expect Building 4-6 have finished upgrading with new chillers.
Land clearing begins for Building 1 (SIN01)

Building 1 (SIN01) is completed, but at this point it likely not being used for AI as the collaboration with Microsoft that resulted in GB300s did not occur until Q1 2026. The completion is based both on satellite imagery, which shows the building fully completed, and a [March 2025 article](https://econews.pt/2025/03/24/start-campus-to-start-construction-of-second-data-centre-building-in-sines-by-end-june/) that says it "has been in operation since October", which is October of 2024.

Building 1 (SIN01) operational for AI, with the first deployment of 12,600 Nvidia GB300 NVL72s to the site being reported completed "Q1 2026" based on the [corporate announcement](https://lp.startcampus.pt/start-campus-hosts-eus-first-nvidia-gb300-chips-with-nscale-for-microsoft). Land clearing also begins for Building 2 (SIN02), with faint dirt outlines for roads and building foundations visible.

Building 2 (SIN02) projected to be operational. This is based on the timeline provided in the [factsheet](https://23712035.hs-sites.com/hubfs/SINES_02.PDF.pdf?hsCtaAttrib=199407154715) for the site. We expect VR200 GPUs to be used as a lot of those chips will have already been available in September 2027, and the "up to 600kW racks" in the factsheet suggest they are aiming for this.
Building 1 is operational, based on default roof-to-operational time.
Building 2 is being retrofitted from a warehouse. The roof is being painted white, and cooling towers and a new substation are being built just south of the building (based on later imagery).
The new substation for Building 2 looks mostly complete, half of the cooling towers are installed. The black patch in the top left of the building suggests that the new roof is not quite complete.
Building 2 looks almost complete, but still appears to have three cooling towers missing. We therefore expect another 3 months until full operation.
Building 2 is operational (estimate).

Core Scientific, CoreWeave, and Port Muskogee publicly announce groundbreaking for the 100 MW Muskogee HPC data center. Core Scientific [says](https://investors.corescientific.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/99/core-scientific-and-port-muskogee-break-ground-on-100-mw-hpc-data-center) the site will provide about 70 MW of critical IT load for CoreWeave's NVIDIA GPUs.

Based on satellite imagery, Building 1 looks mostly complete, but the site still appears to be in construction or commissioning. Not much progress has happened in the past 4 months, and a Q2 2026 finish seems unlikely.

Based on satellite imagery, Building 1 has completed construction, with adjacent land cleared for what we're assuming will have power substations/generators. We can assume Building 1 is not operational from all the yard work directly surrounding it, and the lack of a parking lot. Building 2 (The one not leased to CoreWeave) is still in land clearing phase.

Estimating building 1 is operational by the end of the year, judging from the building currently completed, the completed power substation to the southwest. The surrounding yard work/construction will likely still take several months, judging from the slow pace.

Core Scientific expects initial delivery of the second, unleased 82.5 MW Muskogee building in Q4 2027. Not signed to have CoreWeave GPUs as of yet, but may change in the future. Source: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1839341/000162828026030918/exhibit991polarispressre.htm

Building 2 operational. The completion date for [Building 2 Tenant Improvement](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025027065) is 2026-11-07, about 2 months later than Building 1. We therefore add 2 months to the Building 1 operational date.

6 buildings are operational. This is based on:
1. A [regulatory filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2026010861) for Building 6 with a completion date of 2027-07-30. Normally there is a follow-up filing for "tenant improvement" which involves building the interior, with a completion date 1-3 months after the initial filing.
2. A project deadline of EOY 2027 in the [original tax abatement agreement](https://www.co.armstrong.tx.us/upload/page/5361/Phase%201%20Tax%20Abatement%20Agreement%20-%20Signed.pdf)
3. The similar 8-building project in Abilene taking an estimated 2 years and 2 months.

New [power stations](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1D4eUHwEuirNi0g3IU_xGOti3PcC8fcXi/view?usp=sharing) are in service for a 1.8 GW load at Holly Ridge. However, the data center is still not operational.

First phase is operational, based on a [Meta blog post](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1PmFNgOwkPepwjpGIbNUuPJP0LNgIqe7l/view?usp=sharing). We assume that this aligns with the three natural gas turbines being completed, for a total of 2262 MW capacity, but that these turbines have a backup role. Meta [claims](https://engineering.fb.com/2025/09/29/data-infrastructure/metas-infrastructure-evolution-and-the-advent-of-ai/) that "The Hyperion cluster will have a 5-gigawatt capacity once complete", but so far there is no concrete evidence for this happening at the Holly Ridge site alone.

All buildings finished, site is fully operational. Our reasoning is that examples so far take a median of 2-2.5 years to go from land clearing to 1 GW. This suggests around 2028-01-01 here. However, due to delays being more likely than speedups and the fact that this base rate data is still unproven, the more conservative estimate is made. The current state of the site also supports this estimation.

Building 1 and 2 are operational. The similar data center in Ajman, UAE was delayed by over a year, now due in December 2026 (compare [1](https://khaznadatacenters.com/press-release/khazna-expands-infrastructure-to-accelerate-uaes-leadership-in-artificial-intelligence/), [2](https://khaznadatacenters.com/press-release/khazna-announces-the-uaes-first-ai-optimized-data-center-and-the-largest-in-its-portfolio-powering-the-nations-drive-toward-ai-leadership/)). The buildings here looked 3-6 months behind the Ajman building as of November 2025, but they were moving faster. So we think Building 1 and 2 are on track for 200 MW by December 2026, along with the Ajman building.

1 GW of IT power is operational. We haven’t seen expansion beyond 200 MW as of November 2025. Optimistically, 8 more 100 MW buildings start construction in December 2025, in parallel, and take 1.5 years like the first two buildings, hitting 1 GW by Q3 2027. More realistically, there's some staggering of construction over several months, like Stargate Abilene. Our median estimate is January 2028.

Building 1 site is cleared/vacant land; no construction visible yet (based on Google Earth historical imagery).

Satellite imagery shows 30 fully installed air-cooled chillers (20 fans each) on the roof, with room for many more. In the same month, Firmus [reported](https://firmus.co/newsroom/southgate-expansion) that Project Southgate's "first stages are under construction in Tasmania and Melbourne". So we don't think the Southgate part of the building is operational yet.

Building 1's cooling capacity increased to 53 air-cooled chillers (20 fans each), up from 30 in Oct 2025. This is more than enough for the announced 18,400 GB300 GPUs, but construction does not quite look complete, so we expect another 3 months until it's operational.

Building 1 is operational (estimate). No additional chip deployment beyond the 18,400 GB300 GPUs has been publicly confirmed, so we continue to report the disclosed capacity allocated to Project Southgate, rather than speculate on further buildout.
